title: Elaphandra macrolepis
intro: Elaphandra macrolepis, commonly known as the "Peruvian snakeroot," is a plant species in the family Apiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, pain, and inflammation due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. The bioactive constituents of Elaphandra macrolepis include sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and rheumatic pain.
